items.title: Thinking Machines released Inkling with open weights
items.oneliner: Inkling ships with 975B total parameters, 41B active, and 1M context; open multimodal weights just got more serious.

items.title: OpenAI put GPT-Red into model training
items.oneliner: OpenAI says GPT-Red helped train GPT-5.6 against prompt injection, turning red teaming into a model-to-model pipeline.

items.title: xAI open-sourced Grok Build
items.oneliner: xAI open-sourced its terminal coding agent after privacy questions, signaling that developer trust now requires inspectable tooling.

items.title: Weco showed early recursive self-improvement
items.oneliner: Weco says AIDE2 tested 100 self-rewrites and kept the best ones; agent improvement loops are leaving theory.

items.title: Suno breach exposed training data practices
items.oneliner: A Suno hack leaked source code and evidence of scraping YouTube, Deezer, and Genius; provenance risk is now operational.

items.title: Microsoft confirmed an undeletable Windows identifier
items.oneliner: Microsoft confirmed Windows assigns a persistent GDID tied to Microsoft accounts; device tracking is now a platform feature.

items.title: Forgejo shipped an SSRF hardening release
items.oneliner: Forgejo 16.0 tightened Git mirroring against SSRF and warned admins about trusted proxies; self-hosting still demands real ops discipline.

items.title: Patchless tracks missing kernel security fixes
items.oneliner: Patchless scans upstream Linux commits to flag likely unpropagated security patches, giving defenders earlier visibility than distro advisories.

items.title: High-bandwidth flash targets model weight bottlenecks
items.oneliner: High-bandwidth flash could cut latency and energy for model-weight reads, which matters more as inference becomes memory-bound.

items.title: Stripe reportedly bid more than $53 billion
items.oneliner: Stripe and Advent reportedly offered over $53 billion for PayPal, a consolidation move that could reshape payments infrastructure.
